title: Crustal Structure of the Incoming Iquique Ridge Offshore Northern Chile
journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
abstract: The crustal structure and thickness of the Iquique Ridge is determined using multiple coincident seismic imaging techniques from the 2016 PICTURES experiment (MGL1610). The Iquique Ridge is characterized by significant crustal thickening relative to typical Nazca plate oceanic crust, with a maximum thickness of approximately 13 km. Multi-channel seismic reflection data show along-profile structural variation including an intermittent Moho reflector near the onset of crustal thickening. P-wave velocity models are derived from 2D tomographic inversions of travel time data. Reduced upper crustal velocities extending toward the trench suggest enhanced fracturing and hydration during plate bending.

location.description: Incoming Nazca plate, Iquique Ridge offshore northern Chile (~20°S), seaward of the trench

seismic_lines.name: PICTURES wide-angle OBS + MCS profile across Iquique Ridge (~20°S)

data.notes: 51 OBS stations (XW network, 2016) deployed during MGL1610 cruise offshore northern Chile. Wide-angle seismic refraction data used for 2D P-wave travel time tomography of Iquique Ridge crustal structure.
MCS reflection data from PICTURES cruise (MGL1610, 2016) targeting the Iquique Ridge, showing Moho reflector and crustal structure. Maximum crustal thickness ~13 km identified.
Raw multibeam swath bathymetry, backscatter, and sidescan data (2,642 files, ~170 GB) acquired with Kongsberg EM122 during the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise offshore northern Chile 19°–21°S.
Raw underway gravimeter data (Bell BGM-3, 48 files, ~167 MB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise. A processed gravity product (~696 MB) is also available via RVData.
Raw magnetometer data (Geometrics G-882, 65 files, ~2.6 GB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise. A processed magnetics product (~2.5 GB) is also available.
Primary navigation (GPS positions) data from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise, C-Nav 3050 system, archived via Rolling Deck to Repository (R2R).
Processed seismic shot time navigation data from the MGL1610 PICTURES cruise, offshore Chile. For use when processing the MCS reflection data.
Raw singlebeam/subbottom profiler data (Knudsen 3260, 50 files, ~1.1 GB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise.

processing.summary: Both MCS reflection and wide-angle OBS seismic data from the PICTURES cruise (MGL1610, 2016) processed to characterize the crustal structure of the Iquique Ridge on the incoming Nazca plate. OBS data processed through 2D P-wave travel time tomography. MCS data processed through standard workflow for reflection imaging. Results reveal maximum crustal thickness of ~13 km, reduced upper crustal velocities indicating fracturing/hydration, and intermittent Moho reflector.
workflow: 1. MGL1610 OBS data loading and phase picking
3. MCS: geometry, filter, velocity analysis, stack, migration
4. Moho reflector identification on MCS sections
processing.notes: Closed access in JGR Solid Earth. Myers (OSU PhD student), Tréhu (OSU), Contreras-Reyes (U. Chile). Uses PICTURES (MGL1610, 2016) data.

analysis_notes: Paper behind paywall; no open access version found. Abstract reconstructed from OpenAlex inverted index and Semantic Scholar summary. PICTURES (MGL1610, 2016) cruise confirmed. Tréhu (OSU) and Contreras-Reyes confirmed from author list. All MGL1610 cruise data confirmed open access at MGDS/RVData (cruise DOI 10.7284/907099).
